---
title: "The Story of Scurvy"
subtitle: "How we lost and rediscovered the cure"
format:
  html: default
execute:
  echo: false
  eval: true
---

# The Story of Scurvy

Scurvy was a devastating disease that afflicted sailors on long voyages. In **1747**, James Lind showed with a controlled experiment that **lemons prevent scurvy**—a correct finding, but the *why* was still unknown. Over time, people came to believe it was the **acid** in lemons that killed the bacteria causing scurvy. That wrong idea led to replacing lemons with limes or vinegar (cheaper, but far less effective), and scurvy returned. Only in **1928** was the true mechanism found: **Vitamin C** (ascorbic acid) prevents scurvy. That complete understanding finally explained why lemons worked and why the acid theory failed.

The three diagrams below show these three different views of the same data-generating process: first the incomplete but correct one (lemons → scurvy prevention), then the mistaken causal chain (acid → bacteria death → scurvy prevention), and finally the correct mechanism (vitamin C → scurvy prevention).

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false

import daft
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

plt.rcParams['figure.dpi'] = 150
plt.rcParams['savefig.dpi'] = 300

def draw_arrow(pgm, from_node, to_node):
    """Draw a visible arrowhead on top of rendered nodes."""
    ax  = pgm.ax
    ctx = pgm._ctx
    n1, n2 = pgm._nodes[from_node], pgm._nodes[to_node]
    start = n1.get_frontier_coord(ctx.convert(n2.x, n2.y), ctx, None)
    end   = n2.get_frontier_coord(ctx.convert(n1.x, n1.y), ctx, None)
    ax.annotate(
        "", xy=end, xytext=start,
        arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->", color="k", lw=2,
                        connectionstyle="arc3,rad=0"),
    )
```

## DAG 1: The 1747 Understanding (Correct but Incomplete)

Lemons prevent scurvy—Lind had the right treatment, but not the underlying cause.

```{python}
#| label: fig-dag-1747
#| fig-cap: "DAG 1: Lemons prevent scurvy (1747 — correct but incomplete)"
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 2.5

pgm = daft.PGM(dpi=150, alternate_style="outer", directed=True)

pgm.add_node("lemons", "Lemons", 1, 1, aspect=3, scale=1.2,
             plot_params={
                 "facecolor": "lightyellow",
                 "edgecolor": "darkgoldenrod",
                 "linewidth": 2,
                 "alpha": 0.9,
             })
pgm.add_node("scurvy_prevention", "Scurvy\nPrevention", 3.25, 1, aspect=3, scale=1.2,
             plot_params={
                 "facecolor": "lightgreen",
                 "edgecolor": "darkgreen",
                 "linewidth": 2,
                 "alpha": 0.9,
             })
pgm.add_edge("lemons", "scurvy_prevention", directed=False,
             plot_params={"color": "k", "linewidth": 2})
pgm.render()
draw_arrow(pgm, "lemons", "scurvy_prevention")
plt.show()
```

## DAG 2: The Misguided Belief (Wrong)

People thought acid killed the bacteria that caused scurvy—so they swapped lemons for limes or vinegar and lost the benefit.

```{python}
#| label: fig-dag-misguided
#| fig-cap: "DAG 2: Acid kills bacteria that cause scurvy (misguided belief)"
#| fig-width: 4
#| fig-height: 5

pgm = daft.PGM(dpi=150, alternate_style="outer", directed=True)

pgm.add_node("acid", "Acid", 1, 2, aspect=2.5, scale=1.2,
             plot_params={
                 "facecolor": "mistyrose",
                 "edgecolor": "indianred",
                 "linewidth": 2,
                 "alpha": 0.9,
             })
pgm.add_node("bacteria_death", "Bacteria\nDeath", 1, 1, aspect=2.5, scale=1.2,
             plot_params={
                 "facecolor": "lavenderblush",
                 "edgecolor": "crimson",
                 "linewidth": 2,
                 "alpha": 0.9,
             })
pgm.add_node("scurvy_prevention", "Scurvy\nPrevention", 1, 0, aspect=2.5, scale=1.2,
             plot_params={
                 "facecolor": "lightgreen",
                 "edgecolor": "darkgreen",
                 "linewidth": 2,
                 "alpha": 0.9,
             })
pgm.add_edge("acid", "bacteria_death", directed=False,
             plot_params={"color": "k", "linewidth": 2})
pgm.add_edge("bacteria_death", "scurvy_prevention", directed=False,
             plot_params={"color": "k", "linewidth": 2})
pgm.render()
draw_arrow(pgm, "acid", "bacteria_death")
draw_arrow(pgm, "bacteria_death", "scurvy_prevention")
plt.show()
```

## DAG 3: The 1928 Understanding (Complete and Correct)

Vitamin C prevents scurvy—the true mechanism that explains why lemons worked and why the acid story was wrong.

```{python}
#| label: fig-dag-1928
#| fig-cap: "DAG 3: Vitamin C prevents scurvy (1928 — complete and correct)"
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 2.5

pgm = daft.PGM(dpi=150, alternate_style="outer", directed=True)

pgm.add_node("vitamin_c", "Vitamin C", 1, 1, aspect=3, scale=1.2,
             plot_params={
                 "facecolor": "lightcyan",
                 "edgecolor": "teal",
                 "linewidth": 2,
                 "alpha": 0.9,
             })
pgm.add_node("scurvy_prevention", "Scurvy\nPrevention", 3.25, 1, aspect=3, scale=1.2,
             plot_params={
                 "facecolor": "lightgreen",
                 "edgecolor": "darkgreen",
                 "linewidth": 2,
                 "alpha": 0.9,
             })
pgm.add_edge("vitamin_c", "scurvy_prevention", directed=False,
             plot_params={"color": "k", "linewidth": 2})
pgm.render()
draw_arrow(pgm, "vitamin_c", "scurvy_prevention")
plt.show()
```
